---
title: "Tvary mýdlových bublin"
date: "2025-12-08"
updated: "2026-01-25"
description: "Jaké tvary mají mýdlové bubliny když se vzájemně dotýkají?"
draft: true
categories: ["Přednáška 10", "Bubliny"]
# image: "image.jpg"
pdflink: "[![PDF](images/pdf_icon.png){width=60px}](posts/p10_soap_opera/geometrie_bublin.pdf)"

# html-math-method: katex
engine: julia

lang: cs

format:
    typst:
        output-file: "geometrie_bublin"
        output-ext: "pdf"
        engine: typst

---

# Jaké tvary mají mýdlové bubliny když se vzájemně dotýkají a jak to souvisí s geometrií a fyzikou?

## Co říkají Plateauovy zákony

Pro **rovnovážné** mýdlové filmy / bubliny platí:

- **Tři plochy** (filmy) se setkávají podél jedné křivky (Plateauův okraj) **vždy po třech** a **vždy pod úhlem 120°** mezi každými dvěma.   
- **Čtyři takové hrany** se pak setkávají v jednom bodě pod tzv. **tetraedrickým úhlem** \(\arccos(-1/3) \approx 109{,}47^\circ\).   
- Každá jednotlivá plocha má **konstantní střední křivost** (rovnováha mezi tlakem a povrchovým napětím).   

Jinými slovy: konfigurace, kde se plochy potkávají jinak než v 120°/109.47°, je **nestabilní** a film se přeuspořádá.

<!-- 


The Young–Laplace equation for minimal surfaces describes the equilibrium of a surface with zero mean curvature, where the pressure difference (
) across the surface is zero (
), implying the surface minimizes area, as in soap films. The equation is defined as 
, where 
 are principal radii of curvature. 
Wikipedia

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Young%E2%80%93Laplace_equation

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Minimal_surface

Key Aspects of the Minimal Surface Equation
Mean Curvature: A surface is minimal if its mean curvature 
 is zero at every point, meaning the principal curvatures are equal in magnitude but opposite in sign (
).
Physical Meaning: The equation, derived from the Wikipedia Young–Laplace equation (
), states that for a soap film with equal pressure on both sides (
), the mean curvature 
 must be zero.
PDE Representation: For a surface defined as the graph of a function 
, the minimal surface equation is a second-order nonlinear partial differential equation:

.
Geometric Property: Every point on a minimal surface is a saddle point (negative Gaussian curvature, 
).
Examples: Common examples include the catenoid, helicoid, and Enneper surface. 
Wikipedia
Wikipedia
 +4
The equation was formulated by Lagrange in 1762 as part of solving the Scribd Plateau problem. 


https://www.sciencedirect.com/topics/mathematics/minimal-surface-equation

https://www.scribd.com/document/246099961/Minimal-Surface 


https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Euler%E2%80%93Lagrange_equation
-->
